name: RANTON: RASEN ARASHI (STORM RELEASE: SPIRALING TEMPEST)

description.value:
Similar to the Rasengan technique, the user is required to form a ball of whirling chakra in his hand and thrust it towards the enemy. However, unlike the Rasengan, this whirling mass of chakra will appear watery with a pulsing electric nucleus. The user makes a melee touch attack with the Rasen Arashi that, if it connects, deals 8d8 force damage, 4d6 water damage, 4d4 electricity damage and it will push the target backwards a number of feet equal to half the damage, rounded to the nearest 5-foot unit. The target must succeed a Fortitude save or else fall prone and become stunned for one round.

Using this technique will only form the Rasen Arashi. Attacking with it is an attack action, but can also be part of a full-attack action. The technique will be discharged when used, regardless whether it hit or not. The user can hold onto it for 1 round per level before it dissipates, if not used beforehand.

Requires: Chakra Control 18 ranks, Electric, Water Affinity

Effect: Creates 1 rasen arashi

Empower: The user may also spend 3 points of chakra to increase the damage by 1d8, 1d6 and 1d4. (Maximum 1 die each per level or 16d8, 12d6 and 12d4.)

Mastery: At fifth step of mastery, the user is able to maintain the Rasen Arashi for a short period of time to use as a weapon. For 1 round per 4 levels, the user may make a Concentration check (DC 25) after attacking with the Rasen Arashi to maintain its form. The damage dealt by the technique is halved although targets hit by the technique are still push backwards and must succeed the Fortitude save as mentioned above. Each subsequent round after the first attack costs an additional 2 chakra. The technique may not be empowered when used in this fashion.
